name: Practically Fat-Free York Gingerbread (Ovo La
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Firmly packed light brown sugar
1 cup Carrots - pureed cooked or canned
3/4 cup Egg substitute, frozen and thawed
1/2 cup Fresh ginger root, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Spray a 13-by-9-by-2-inch baking pan with non-stick vegetable spray and set aside.
Churn orange zest and sugar in food processor for 30 seconds, scrape bowl sides down, and churn for another 30 seconds until zest is finely grated.
Add flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, and nutmeg to the food processor and pulse for 10-12 times to combine. Transfer to a large mixing bowl.
Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients. Buzz buttermilk, carrots, molasses, and lemon juice in the food processor for 5 seconds.
Add egg substitute and ginger to the food processor and pulse for 3-4 times.
Gently fold the carrot mixture into the well using a large rubber spatula, as if folding beaten egg whites.
The batter should be lumpy; a few dry flecks are okay and will disappear during baking. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan, spreading to the corners and smoothing the top.
Sprinkle evenly with cinnamon-sugar topping (1 1/2 tablespoons sugar mixed with 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon).
Bake for 35-40 minutes until the cake is springy to touch and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool the cake at room temperature on a wire rack, then cut into 24 bars by making two cuts lengthwise across the pan and seven crosswise.
